core_behavior: The system must accept, validate, authorize, capture, and record monetary payment transactions on behalf of users or automated processes.
The system must ensure that all payment data is handled in a manner that protects confidentiality, integrity, and availability throughout the full transaction lifecycle.
A payment request is submitted with all required parameters (Initiation stage).
Input data is verified for completeness, format correctness, and business rule compliance (Validation stage).
The payment is submitted to the appropriate payment processor or gateway for approval (Authorization stage).
Authorized funds are captured (immediately or deferred, depending on configuration) (Capture stage).
Funds are transferred and the transaction is finalized (Settlement stage).
A complete, tamper-evident audit record is written (Recording stage).
The initiating party and, where applicable, the payer receive confirmation or failure notice (Notification stage).
The system must support credit card payments (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover).
The system must support debit card payments.
The system must support ACH / bank transfer payments.
The system must support digital wallet payments (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay).
inputs: payment_method_type: Enum; CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, ACH, DIGITAL_WALLET
amount: Decimal; > 0.00; max 12 digits including 2 decimal places
currency: String; ISO 4217 three-letter code (e.g., USD, EUR)
cardholder_name: String; 2–100 characters; printable ASCII
card_number: String; 13–19 digits; transmitted only over TLS; never stored post-tokenization
expiry_month: Integer; 1–12
expiry_year: Integer; current year to current year + 20
cvv: String; 3–4 digits; never stored after authorization attempt
billing_address: Object; see billing address fields
merchant_id: UUID; must match an active, authenticated merchant account
idempotency_key: UUID v4; client-generated; used to prevent duplicate charges
routing_number: String; 9 digits; ABA format; validated via checksum
account_number: String; 4–17 digits
account_type: Enum; CHECKING, SAVINGS
account_holder_name: String; 2–100 characters
billing address line1: String; 1–100 characters
billing address line2: String; optional; 0–100 characters
billing address city: String; 1–50 characters
billing address state_province: String; ISO 3166-2 subdivision code
billing address postal_code: String; format validated per country
billing address country: String; ISO 3166-1 alpha-2
order_id: optional; merchant-supplied reference string; max 64 characters
customer_id: optional; reference to a stored customer profile
capture_mode: optional; IMMEDIATE (default) or DEFERRED
statement_descriptor: optional; string shown on payer's statement; max 22 characters; alphanumeric + spaces
metadata: optional; key-value map; max 20 keys; keys and values max 64 characters each
security_constraints: cvv never stored after authorization attempt
The system must ensure that all payment data is handled in a manner that protects confidentiality, integrity, and availability throughout the full transaction lifecycle.
A complete, tamper-evident audit record is written.
